purpose: Contributor-editable role-lensed mechanic advice for MythicIQ dangerous mechanics. Advice objects may include generic/tank/healer/dps; missing role fields simply do not render.

confidenceMeaning.high: Mechanic handling cross-checked against current dungeon guide text or existing curated note.
confidenceMeaning.medium: Mechanic-specific advice from guide context, curated note, or strong spell/context inference.
confidenceMeaning.low: Name/archetype inference; keep but review before showing as authoritative.

sourcesSummary.algethar_academy: Method Algeth’ar Academy guide and Wowhead Algeth’ar Academy strategy for Germinate/Overgrown Ancient handling.
sourcesSummary.magisters_terrace: Method Magister’s Terrace Midnight Season 1 guide.
sourcesSummary.maisara_caverns: Method Maisara Caverns Midnight Season 1 guide.
sourcesSummary.nexus_point_xenas: Method Nexus-Point Xenas Midnight Season 1 guide and Icy Veins NPX role notes.
sourcesSummary.pit_of_saron: Method Pit of Saron Midnight Season 1 guide and Wowhead overview.
sourcesSummary.seat_of_the_triumvirate: Method Seat of the Triumvirate Midnight Season 1 guide plus cross-check on Chains handling.
sourcesSummary.skyreach: Icy Veins Skyreach Midnight Season 1 guide plus Wowhead overview snippets.
sourcesSummary.windrunner_spire: Method Windrunner Spire Midnight Season 1 guide plus Wowhead overview snippets.

advice.generic: Energy Bomb targets a player; loosely spread so the explosion does not cleave allies and use a defensive if it overlaps other random damage.
advice.tank: Keep the boss stable while the group spreads; do not drag people through rifts during the bomb.
advice.healer: Watch for Energy Bomb plus Arcane Missiles combos; the target may need spot healing before the bomb resolves.
advice.dps: If targeted, step out just far enough to avoid splashing allies, then return.

advice.generic: Firestorm starts after the fire goal and adds pulsing damage plus circles; dodge continuously and use personals during overlaps.
advice.tank: Keep the boss predictable so players can dodge fire circles without losing orientation.
advice.healer: Plan healing for sustained fire pulses after the goal is scored.
advice.dps: Save damage cooldowns for the boss amp if assigned, but do not tunnel through circle spawns.

advice.generic: Gale Force adds pushback/tornado pressure after scoring the air goal; keep your back and movement path safe.
advice.tank: Avoid positioning the boss where pushback sends players off the platform or through tornadoes.
advice.healer: Be ready for movement-heavy healing while players are being pushed around.
advice.dps: Move with the wind, not against it; prioritize survival over standing still for uptime.

advice.generic: Loosely spread for Deafening Screech so the hit/DoT does not chain into stacked allies; use defensives if health is low.
advice.tank: Do not over-stack the boss during this cast; make room for melee to spread without losing uptime.
advice.healer: Plan group healing for the hit and follow-up DoT, especially after ball/goal phases.
advice.dps: Respect the spread even if it costs a cast; clipping others turns this into healer panic.

advice.generic: Run out of the Expel Intruders radius or line of sight it; do not greed casts inside the circle.
advice.tank: Avoid pinning the group against terrain where they cannot line of sight or exit the AoE.
advice.healer: Expect avoidable hits when people are trapped by positioning; stabilize anyone late leaving.
advice.dps: Cancel casts and move early; use movement before the cast finishes, not after the hit.

advice.generic: Deadly Winds targets a player and leaves a tornado; drop it away from the pack path and keep moving clear of the tornado afterward.
advice.tank: Keep mobs positioned so tornadoes do not cut off melee or the next movement path.
advice.healer: Watch the targeted player if it overlaps with other group damage.
advice.dps: If targeted, place the tornado out of cleave space and return quickly.

advice.generic: Mana Bombs are targeted arcane explosions; spread before they land and avoid overlapping bomb circles.
advice.healer: Watch bomb targets for follow-up damage if they are slow to spread.
advice.dps: Step out with your circle and return after it resolves; do not drag the circle through melee.

advice.generic: Arcane Fissure leaves dangerous arcane lines/zones; sidestep the line and do not fight on top of fissure paths.
advice.tank: Turn or move enemies so melee are not forced to stand in fissures to hit the target.
advice.healer: Expect avoidable damage from players slow to sidestep.
advice.dps: Move perpendicular to the fissure rather than running along it.

advice.generic: Swap to the Ancient Branch immediately, interrupt Healing Touch, then stand in the Abundance circle after it dies to clear Splinterbark.
advice.tank: Keep the boss and branch positioned so melee can cleave without dragging the group out of the safe Abundance circle.
advice.healer: Track Splinterbark and expect players to use the Abundance circle rather than spending dispels inefficiently.
advice.dps: Hard swap to the branch and interrupt Healing Touch; do not tunnel the boss while the branch is alive.

advice.generic: Stack tightly on or near the tank, then rotate together around the boss during the Germinate channel. This keeps Hungry Lashers spawning in one clump while everyone dodges the swirlies.
advice.tank: Lead the rotation path around the boss and keep the lashers close enough to cleave. After Burst Forth, pick up active lashers quickly and be ready to kite if poison stacks climb.
advice.healer: Expect poison and burst pressure when lashers activate; rotate poison dispels where available and be ready for group healing after Burst Forth.
advice.dps: Cleave the lashers aggressively and use AoE stops/slows if they activate before dying.

advice.generic: Power Vacuum pulls everyone to the boss; use movement immediately and avoid being pulled into Arcane Rifts.
advice.tank: Position the boss so the pull path does not drag the group through existing rifts; be ready for tank hits around the same timing.
advice.healer: Pre-position and be ready for anyone pulled through rifts or late escaping.
advice.dps: Use mobility as soon as the pull starts and do not save movement for after you are already in danger.

advice.generic: Mana Void targets one player; spread enough that the hit does not splash nearby allies.
advice.tank: Avoid dragging the pack through players spreading for Mana Void.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target and watch for overlaps with Arcane Missiles or other random damage.
advice.dps: Do not stack on the target; use a personal if already low or targeted during other damage.

advice.generic: Arcane Rifts persist and fire orbs; place new rifts near the room edge when possible and keep lanes open for dodging.
advice.tank: Pull the boss toward the entrance/edge early and avoid dropping tank-rift stacks in the group path.
advice.healer: Track players at 2 Overwhelming Power stacks; they may need help while placing their next rift safely.
advice.dps: If you are about to spawn a rift, step out and place it in a planned area instead of under the group.

advice.generic: Stay close enough to the pack that Vicious Ambush does not drag the mob far away; the farthest player should be ready for the leap and follow-up frontal pattern.
advice.tank: After the leap, expect the frontal to point back toward you; sidestep the Rift Breath and quickly re-center the mob.
advice.healer: Watch the ambush target for a burst hit, especially if they were baiting far.
advice.dps: Do not bait this across the room; keep it cleavable and sidestep the follow-up frontal.

advice.generic: Seed Detonation indicates lashers or seed effects are about to become dangerous; be away from dormant spawns and ready to swap/control.
advice.tank: Pick up newly active lashers and avoid tanking them during another buster without mitigation.
advice.healer: Expect a damage spike when seeds activate; prepare group healing/poison dispels.
advice.dps: Finish dormant/low-health adds before they become active, then stun/cleave the rest.

advice.generic: Unleash Energy happens immediately on pull and creates the first rifts; start the fight topped and already moving to the planned boss position.
advice.tank: Pull toward the entrance/edge so the first rifts do not consume the middle of the room.
advice.healer: Top the group before the pull and prepare for early damage before normal ramp tools are established.
advice.dps: Do not open from the middle if the group is moving the boss out; follow the tank and keep the center clean.

advice.generic: Astral Whirlwind spawns an AoE under a player; move immediately and avoid dropping it in the group path.
advice.tank: Keep the pull mobile enough that players can leave the AoE without breaking the pack.
advice.healer: Spot heal players who get trapped by overlapping whirls.
advice.dps: If targeted, step out and keep the floor clear for melee/tank movement.

advice.generic: Arcane Expulsion knocks the group and drops a long-lasting puddle under the boss; place it on edges or overlap old puddles to preserve room.
advice.tank: Move the boss so each Expulsion drops at the edge/old puddles and use terrain to manage knockbacks.
advice.healer: Top players before Expulsion and be ready if Shackles overlaps with the knockback.
advice.dps: Do not stand between the boss and bad space; recover quickly after the knockback.

advice.generic: Void Torrent splits the arena with a beam; avoid the beam and pre-split the group so both sides can catch Unstable Void Essence soaks.
advice.tank: Keep your position predictable so both sides of the beam remain playable.
advice.healer: Be ready to heal both sides of the split; line/range may matter while the beam is active.
advice.dps: Respect the pre-split and catch your side’s soaks instead of crossing late through the beam.

advice.generic: If targeted by Astral Grasp, run away from the pull-in and avoid the circles appearing around clones.
advice.healer: Watch the grasp target during the pull and clone-circle overlap.
advice.dps: Move early; do not wait until the pull has already dragged you into circles.

advice.generic: Suppression Zone is used intentionally for Wave of Silence, but it silences/pacifies while inside. Step in just before the cast finishes, then leave.
advice.tank: Place the zone near center so Runic Mark targets can clear while spread.
advice.healer: Time instant healing before stepping into the zone; you cannot cast while silenced/pacified.
advice.dps: Do not sit inside the zone early; enter late to dodge Wave of Silence, then exit.

advice.generic: Runic Mark targets should clear in Suppression Zone while staying separated from each other so Null Reaction does not double-hit the group.
advice.tank: Keep the boss/zone near the center so both targets can clear without crossing the group.
advice.healer: The second Runic Mark clear may need extra healing or a defensive because Null Reaction can chain with other damage.
advice.dps: If marked, move decisively to the zone and leave a gap from the other marked player.

advice.generic: Unstable Energy stacks from soaking Energy Orbs; soak what is needed but use defensives and do not chain too many stacks unnecessarily.
advice.healer: Expect heavy spot healing during orb soaking and watch players with high Unstable Energy stacks.
advice.dps: Use damage cooldowns during Refueling Protocol, soak assigned orbs, then move out before stacks become dangerous.

advice.generic: Null Reaction is the punishment when Runic Mark is cleared; stagger/space the clears so multiple hits do not stack on the party.
advice.healer: Prepare spot healing for the second clear and call if players are clearing too close together.
advice.dps: Marked players should not both clear on top of each other; leave room before removing the mark.

advice.generic: Void Bomb targets a player with a healing absorb/splash; spread from allies and help remove the absorb with self-healing if possible.
advice.healer: Heal the absorb off quickly, especially if multiple Void Bombs or Devouring Strike overlap.
advice.dps: If targeted, step away from allies and use defensives/self-healing to help clear the absorb.

advice.generic: Flamestrike creates an AoE hit and puddle; move out before impact and do not drop it in the route path.
advice.healer: Be ready when Flamestrike overlaps Ignition or other library trash damage.
advice.dps: Interrupt Pyroblast first, but still move out of Flamestrike immediately.

advice.generic: Hulking Fragment hits the tank and applies a Magic DoT/puddle pattern; the tank should move out before dispel so the puddle drops away from melee.
advice.tank: Aim the hit away from allies, back away after the hit, and call for dispel once you are out of melee space.
advice.healer: Wait until the tank is out before dispelling so the puddle lands away from the group.
advice.dps: Give the tank room to move out; do not chase into the dispel puddle drop zone.

advice.generic: Arcane Beam tracks/channels into a player and leaves puddles; guide it away from the group path and avoid the trail.
advice.tank: Keep enemies positioned so the beam target has a safe path out and melee are not boxed in.
advice.healer: Watch the beam target while they move; the danger is often beam plus knockback/other puddles.
advice.dps: If targeted, move the beam away from allies and avoid painting the group path.

advice.generic: Cosmic Sting drops a puddle under the target; place it away from the center and away from linked movement paths.
advice.tank: Keep clones/boss positioned so puddles do not split the arena for melee.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target if puddle movement overlaps other boss focus damage.
advice.dps: If targeted, drop the puddle out of the way and return to cleave quickly.

advice.generic: Fetid Quillstorm creates circles during Muro’jin/Nekraxx; dodge the circles and avoid standing where future movement will be needed.
advice.healer: Prioritize Infected Pinions dispels while players dodge Quillstorm circles.
advice.dps: Do not tunnel during quill circles; keep bosses cleavable while moving.

advice.generic: Deathgorged Vessel causes passive damage and circles; keep moving out of circles while conserving defensives for overlaps.
advice.healer: Expect ongoing passive damage during Rak’tul and plan healing around circle-heavy moments.
advice.dps: Do not tunnel while circles spawn; uptime is secondary to not adding extra healer load.

advice.generic: Save damage for Necrotic Convergence, break the shield quickly, then interrupt the channel before the ramping damage overwhelms the group.
advice.tank: Hold the boss stable and avoid dragging players away during the shield burn window.
advice.healer: Prepare cooldowns while the shield is up; the damage ramps until the shield is broken and the cast is interrupted.
advice.dps: Pool burst for the shield and swap immediately; this is not a passive cleave moment.

advice.generic: Spiritbreaker is a tank combo that drops a large puddle and knocks back; tank moves away from the group and uses mobility/braziers to handle the knockback.
advice.tank: Use a defensive, move the combo away from the group, and avoid being knocked into bad space by using movement or the braziers.
advice.healer: Track the tank during the knockback/combo and be ready if they land far away or out of line.
advice.dps: Stay clear of the tank drop zone and do not follow the tank into the puddle path.

advice.generic: Kite the Wrest Phantoms adds so they collide with each other and explode, but stagger collisions so the healer can recover between blasts.
advice.tank: Help steer/position adds without letting them all explode at once.
advice.healer: Expect large recovery checks after each collision; call for staggered detonations if the group is low.
advice.dps: Use slows, stuns, and controlled movement to line up collisions one at a time.

advice.generic: Crush Souls targets three players and drops Soulbind Totems; place totems close together without cleaving allies, then kill them fast.
advice.healer: Watch the three targets as they place totems and the follow-up pull begins.
advice.dps: Swap to Soulbind Totems immediately; cleave them down before the pulls create chaos.

advice.generic: Soulbind Totems pull players while channeling; kill the totems quickly and avoid standing in overlapping placement circles.
advice.tank: Keep the boss stable near the totem pile so DPS can swap without losing uptime.
advice.healer: Be ready for players being pulled into danger if totems live too long.
advice.dps: Hard swap to totems; this is a priority mechanic, not passive cleave.

advice.generic: Soulrending Roar sends players into the bridge gauntlet; return quickly, avoid Lost Souls, and interrupt/CC Malignant Souls for the stacking buff.
advice.tank: Keep the boss controlled while players return; avoid forcing extra movement as they re-enter.
advice.healer: Withering Soul ramps while players are away, so be ready to stabilize them when they return.
advice.dps: Move fast through the gauntlet, avoid roots, and interrupt/CC the six Malignant Souls for Spectral Residue.

advice.generic: Eternal Suffering indicates sustained shadow pressure; use personals on overlaps and avoid adding avoidable hits during the window.
advice.healer: Plan efficient healing for sustained ticks rather than waiting for a single burst.
advice.dps: Use a defensive when low or when suffering overlaps with movement mechanics.

advice.generic: Rain of Toads creates avoidable circles; keep moving and avoid being boxed in by other ground effects.
advice.tank: Move the pull so players have room to dodge without spreading mobs everywhere.
advice.healer: Watch players who dodge late while healing absorb effects are active.
advice.dps: Move out early; do not finish a cast in the circle.

advice.generic: Ancestral Crush creates a circle and puddle; avoid the impact and do not fight in the leftover puddle.
advice.tank: Move the mob out of puddles so melee can continue safely.
advice.healer: Spot heal anyone clipped and be ready for passive Spectral Strikes damage in the same pulls.
advice.dps: Sidestep the impact and return after the puddle is clear.

advice.generic: Ritual Firebrand applies Magic debuffs and drops circles; debuffed players move out with circles while the healer dispels as needed.
advice.healer: Dispel Firebrand targets promptly, especially when both debuffs overlap with Hex Guardian pulsing damage.
advice.dps: If debuffed, move your circle away from the group and use a defensive until dispelled.

advice.generic: Soulstorms rotate around the Bound Defender; keep moving with the pattern rather than cutting through it.
advice.tank: Face the mob away for Vigilant Defense and position it so storms leave melee a safe arc.
advice.healer: Expect avoidable hits when players are forced to move through rotating storms.
advice.dps: Track the rotation and stay on the safe side of the mob.

advice.generic: During Rend Souls, avoid nearby Lost Souls; the channel can cause them to explode and root/damage players.
advice.tank: Keep the Hollow Soulrender positioned so players are not boxed into Lost Soul paths.
advice.healer: Be ready to dispel/root-break or heal players who get clipped by Lost Souls.
advice.dps: Create space from Lost Souls before the channel resolves; do not chase damage through them.

advice.generic: Umbral Vortex is a pull/zone style danger; move out of the vortex path and use movement early if it is dragging you into bad space.
advice.tank: Avoid tanking mobs where the vortex forces the group through other hazards.
advice.healer: Watch players who spend movement late and get pulled into follow-up damage.
advice.dps: Use movement before you are centered in the vortex.

advice.generic: Freezing Traps are bad unless used to stun Nekraxx during Carrion Swoop; targeted players can intentionally line the swoop through a trap.
advice.tank: Do not get knocked into traps accidentally while managing the bosses together.
advice.healer: Watch the Carrion Swoop target and be ready if the trap setup fails.
advice.dps: If targeted by the swoop, route it through an ice block/trap; everyone else stays out of the path.

advice.generic: Shadow Burst targets a player; spread so it does not cleave allies.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target if Shadow Burst overlaps other trash damage.
advice.dps: Move out with the circle/hit and do not stand on another player.

advice.generic: Frost Nova roots and splashes around the target; move away from allies before it lands and remove the root with dispel/freedom.
advice.healer: Dispel the root quickly when movement is required or when Lost Souls are nearby.
advice.dps: If targeted, spread before impact and use root break/freedom if available.

advice.generic: Suppression Field targets a player; pre-spread so it does not cleave and, if targeted, move as little as possible because movement increases damage taken.
advice.tank: Hold the pack steady so the target can minimize movement without being forced through mobs or puddles.
advice.healer: Watch the target while they are slowed/limited; they may need spot healing if forced to move.
advice.dps: If targeted, stop unnecessary movement and let the group move away from you.

advice.generic: Eclipsing Step hits two players; spread enough to avoid cleaving others and use a defensive if low.
advice.healer: Pre-hot/spot heal the two targets and watch the DoT after the hit.
advice.dps: If targeted, move out from allies but stay close enough to maintain group control.

advice.generic: Avoid Leyline Array beams unless you have Reflux Charge; charged players should intentionally stand at beam intersections to disable as many lines as possible.
advice.tank: If a melee has Reflux Charge, position the boss closer to beams so they can clear safely without losing all uptime.
advice.healer: Watch charged players as they intentionally touch beams and may take follow-up damage/stun.
advice.dps: Without Reflux Charge, dodge beams; with Reflux Charge, clear an intersection on purpose.

advice.generic: Dark Beckoning causes pulsing damage and projectiles; dodge the projectiles while using personals if the pulse overlaps other damage.
advice.healer: Prepare healing for the pulsing damage; it can become dangerous with absorbs or Dusk Frights movement.
advice.dps: Maintain movement discipline during the pulse instead of tunneling through projectiles.

advice.generic: Dark Beckoning fires projectiles during pulsing damage; keep moving through safe gaps and avoid adding avoidable hits to the healer check.
advice.healer: Use group healing if players are forced to move while damage pulses.
advice.dps: Dodge projectiles and use a personal if you are targeted by other damage at the same time.

advice.generic: Flailstorm is an AoE/fixate danger; kite if fixated and avoid standing in the storm.
advice.tank: Face the Dreadflail away for Void Lash and be ready to move when Flailstorm begins.
advice.healer: Watch the fixated player while they kite; they may outrange you if they panic.
advice.dps: If fixated, kite through safe space; if not, stay out and keep damage on the mob.

advice.generic: Brilliant Dispersion targets three players and spawns Fractured Images; spread the hits and then avoid the images’ proximity attacks and dash paths.
advice.tank: Keep the boss positioned so images do not trap melee against walls or puddles.
advice.healer: Track the three targets and be ready for image/dash mistakes afterward.
advice.dps: Spread for the hit, then avoid images and do not stand within 5 yards of them.

advice.generic: Searing Rend is tank damage that leaves puddles; tank uses a defensive and drops puddles out of the group path.
advice.tank: Defensive every Searing Rend and move puddles to the edge/out of melee space.
advice.healer: Expect tank spikes plus DoT/puddle movement; pre-position for range.
advice.dps: Do not stand where the tank is dropping Searing Rend puddles.

advice.generic: Erratic Zap creates avoidable circles; keep moving and avoid stacking circles with other players.
advice.healer: Watch for Erratic Zap during Arcing Mana; players may need healing while dodging.
advice.dps: Move out immediately and return to target; do not soak unnecessary circles.

advice.generic: Daze indicates you were clipped by a movement/impact mechanic; use freedom/movement tools and avoid stacking it with follow-up ground effects.
advice.healer: Watch dazed players because they may fail the next movement check.
advice.dps: Clear the snare if possible and reposition before the next beam/puddle pattern.

advice.generic: Corespark Detonation knocks players and creates Flux Collapse/Sparkburn pressure; stay clear of impact, brace for knockback, and bait resulting puddles to the edge.
advice.tank: Position so knockbacks do not throw players into beams or existing puddles.
advice.healer: Plan cooldowns after detonation to remove Sparkburn/healing absorbs quickly.
advice.dps: Use personals if low before detonation; avoid the impact and keep puddles out of the center afterward.

advice.generic: Searing Rend is a tank hit with persistent puddles; keep the puddles organized so the arena remains usable.
advice.tank: Use mitigation and place the puddle away from boss/melee paths.
advice.healer: Watch the tank during the hit and movement after it.
advice.dps: Give the tank a clean lane to drop the puddle.

advice.generic: Flux Collapse creates puddles on players; bait them toward the room edge and keep the center/beam paths open.
advice.tank: Hold the boss so edge baiting is possible and melee are not forced to drop puddles under the boss.
advice.healer: Be ready for damage while players move to edges, especially before Corespark Detonation.
advice.dps: If targeted, place puddles at the edge, not in the center path.

advice.generic: Power Flux creates circles and puddles around Circuit Seer pulls; dodge circles and move away from the puddles left behind.
advice.tank: Do not pin the group between Power Flux puddles and Mana Batteries.
advice.healer: Circuit Seer pulls are healer-heavy; prepare cooldowns if Arcing Mana overlaps with Power Flux movement.
advice.dps: Dodge first, then swap to Mana Batteries if they activate.

advice.generic: Holy Echo should be purged when possible; otherwise expect AoE damage/healing zones on death.
advice.healer: Prepare for extra group damage if Holy Echo is not removed before the mob dies.
advice.dps: Purge the buff if your class can; avoid killing the mob in a position that benefits enemies.

advice.generic: Flux Collapse puddles should be baited toward the edge; do not fill intersections or central movement lanes.
advice.healer: Spot heal bait targets and call if safe space is running out.
advice.dps: Move early to an edge and return after the puddle drops.

advice.generic: Mirrored Rend comes from Fractured Images near them; keep more than 5 yards away and dodge Flicker dash paths.
advice.healer: Spot heal players who get clipped by image proximity/dashes.
advice.dps: Do not chase the boss through images; path around them and continue cleave safely.

advice.generic: Dusk Frights create fear zones; step out early and do not let the zones trap the interrupt path to Nullify.
advice.tank: Position mobs so fear zones do not cover the group’s kick/staging area.
advice.healer: Be ready for players who eat fear during Dark Beckoning or other pulse damage.
advice.dps: Avoid fear zones and keep your interrupt available for Nullify.

advice.generic: Flicker dashes Fractured Images through players; watch image facing/path and avoid standing in the dash line.
advice.healer: Expect sudden spikes when players are clipped by image dashes.
advice.dps: Do not stand between images and their dash destination; reposition before the dash resolves.

advice.generic: Ice Chunk/ore-style impacts create objects or bad ground; avoid the impact and keep usable cover/space intact.
advice.tank: Coordinate which chunk is preserved for boss mechanics.
advice.healer: Watch players who get clipped or stand far from cover.
advice.dps: Do not destroy or drop mechanics on chunks the group still needs.

advice.generic: Leaping Maul cleaves around/near the target; loosely spread so one leap does not hit multiple players.
advice.healer: Spot heal the leap target if it overlaps other physical damage.
advice.dps: Do not stack tightly on trash with leaping geists active.

advice.generic: Blight Splatter leaves puddles; avoid the puddles and soothe Plague Frenzy if available to reduce pressure.
advice.tank: Move the mob out of puddles and call for soothe during enrage.
advice.healer: Avoid standing in splatter while healing through other plague damage.
advice.dps: Soothe enrage if possible and keep damage out of bad ground.

advice.generic: Cryoburst targets all players; loosely spread to avoid cleaving each other, then move as a group away from the puddles spawned.
advice.tank: Move the mob/boss so puddles do not trap the group in the next pattern.
advice.healer: Top players before the group spread and stabilize as they move away.
advice.dps: Spread enough to avoid overlaps, then regroup after puddles are down.

advice.generic: Throw Saronite targets two players; keep circles separated and avoid overlapping Ore Chunks, because destroyed chunks leave large area-denial puddles.
advice.tank: Plan which Ore Chunk you will later destroy with Orebreaker and keep one available for Glacial Overload line-of-sight.
advice.healer: Watch targeted players and remind the group not to destroy all cover before Glacial Overload.
advice.dps: Place your Saronite circle cleanly; do not overlap another target or delete needed cover.

advice.generic: Orebreaker targets the tank; overlap one Ore Chunk to break it and avoid the stun, while preserving another chunk for Glacial Overload.
advice.tank: Use a defensive and deliberately break the assigned Ore Chunk; do not accidentally destroy the last cover.
advice.healer: Tank will spike during Orebreaker; be ready especially if chunk positioning is messy.
advice.dps: Stay clear of the tank/chunk impact and preserve remaining cover.

advice.generic: Glacial Overload requires line of sight behind a remaining Ore Chunk; hide before the channel finishes.
advice.tank: Keep at least one Ore Chunk alive and position the boss/path so everyone can reach it.
advice.healer: Top the group before the channel and dispel Cryostomp/Permeating Cold effects afterward.
advice.dps: Stop tunneling and get behind the chunk early; late movement wipes groups.

advice.generic: Scourgelord’s Brand knocks the tank back, then leaps to that position; tank uses a defensive and moves to avoid the follow-up leap.
advice.tank: Pre-position so the knockback is safe, use a defensive, then move immediately before the leap lands.
advice.healer: Track the tank through the knockback/leap sequence and be ready for range/line issues.
advice.dps: Stay out of the tank’s knockback/leap landing zone.

advice.generic: Rime Blast targets should aim/position to cleave an infused Bone Pile marked by green light, controlling which add spawns later.
advice.tank: Plan where the boss faces/stands so Rime Blast targets can hit the intended Bone Pile safely.
advice.healer: Be ready for players moving into position for the Bone Pile cleave.
advice.dps: If targeted, line the blast through the marked Bone Pile; otherwise avoid the line.

advice.generic: Death’s Grasp creates dodge circles during Tyrannus; move out quickly and keep paths open while handling adds.
advice.tank: Do not tank adds where Death’s Grasp circles cut off the group.
advice.healer: Avoid standing in predictable circle clusters while healing the add wave.
advice.dps: Move first, then resume interrupts/cleave on Plaguespreaders.

advice.generic: Blight Smash is a tank hit that drops a puddle; tank uses a defensive and places the puddle out of the way.
advice.tank: Drop the puddle at the edge or in old bad space, not under the boss path.
advice.healer: Expect tank damage plus group puddle movement immediately after.
advice.dps: Do not stand behind/near the tank drop location.

advice.generic: Plague Expulsion/Explosion creates multiple puddles under players; pre-position along edges and use personals if low.
advice.tank: Keep bosses/adds positioned so players can spread without losing all cleave.
advice.healer: Prepare for group movement and spot healing as four puddles appear.
advice.dps: Place your puddle at the edge or on existing bad if safe; do not fill the center.

advice.generic: During Get 'Em, Ick!, the boss fixates a random player for several seconds; targeted player kites, everyone else maximizes cleave safely.
advice.tank: Be ready to regain control after the fixate and keep the shared-health bosses cleavable.
advice.healer: Watch the fixated player and use externals if they are cornered.
advice.dps: If targeted, kite through safe space; if not targeted, save damage windows for boss cleave.

advice.generic: Ore Chunks are both cover and future puddles; preserve at least one for Glacial Overload and avoid destroying chunks in the middle of the room.
advice.tank: Assign chunk usage for Orebreaker and keep the remaining chunk accessible to the whole group.
advice.dps: Do not cleave or overlap mechanics into chunks the group still needs for cover.

advice.generic: Ice Barrage channels several circles; keep moving and do not overlap pathing with other players.
advice.healer: Watch players who are dodging while diseased/low.
advice.dps: Maintain interrupts on Plaguespreaders while dodging barrage circles.

advice.generic: Dark Rupture creates circles; step out and keep mobs out of the puddled area afterward.
advice.tank: Move the pack after Dark Rupture if the circle lands in melee space.
advice.healer: Spot heal late movers and prepare for tank hits in the same trash pulls.
advice.dps: Do not finish a cast inside Dark Rupture; sidestep and continue.

advice.generic: Frost Breath is a cone/frontal aimed at a player; sidestep the cone and avoid standing in front of the drake.
advice.tank: Face the drake away from the party when possible and be ready for frost-enhanced melees.
advice.healer: Watch random targets if the cone clips them during other trash damage.
advice.dps: Do not stand in front; if targeted, move out of the cone path.

advice.generic: Void Bombs should be cleared by the Phase Dash circle; avoid touching them directly unless assigned with immunity/tank before Overload finishes.
advice.tank: Keep boss/pets stacked and be ready to soak remaining bombs if assigned and safe.
advice.healer: Top the group before Phase Dash/Overload; remaining bombs create extra damage.
advice.dps: Use Phase Dash to clear bombs efficiently and avoid accidental direct contact.

advice.generic: Eruption targets two players with DoT/frontal-style pressure; spread from allies and use a defensive if targeted during other damage.
advice.tank: Do not drag the mob so Eruption targets are forced to spread through frontals.
advice.healer: Spot heal Eruption targets and prepare for DoT overlap.
advice.dps: If targeted, move out from allies and avoid clipping the group.

advice.generic: Decimate leaps to a random player and drops a puddle; place it near the edge or old puddles without dragging the boss too far from melee.
advice.tank: Keep the boss close enough that Decimate targets can place puddles without killing melee uptime.
advice.healer: Spot heal Decimate targets and watch for puddle placement mistakes.
advice.dps: If targeted, step to edge/old bad, drop it cleanly, then return.

advice.generic: Collapsing Void requires everyone to move under the boss after Repulse; use movement immediately and prepare for heavy group damage.
advice.tank: Keep track of the boss center position and help the group collapse under quickly.
advice.healer: Plan a cooldown/group defensive for the channel; movement and damage overlap hard here.
advice.dps: Use mobility to get under the boss; do not run away from the center after Repulse.

advice.generic: Disintegrate creates rotating beams; move with the beams and avoid cutting through them.
advice.tank: Watch Abyssal Lance stacks and use a defensive when the delayed hit is coming while beams rotate.
advice.healer: Heal while rotating and watch players caught by beams.
advice.dps: Keep moving with the beam pattern; small uptime losses beat repeated hits.

advice.generic: Umbral Waves fire from Gates of the Abyss; watch gate locations and move through safe gaps.
advice.tank: Avoid placing the fight where waves cover every route to the boss.
advice.healer: Pre-position so you can heal while dodging waves.
advice.dps: Track the gate spawns before tunneling; dodge waves first.

advice.generic: Void Expulsion follows Rift Tear and causes heavy group damage plus circles; dodge circles and use defensives/healing cooldowns.
advice.tank: Keep Rift Wardens within 30 yards of their rift so Stabilize does not lead to lethal explosions.
advice.healer: Prepare for heavy group damage after Rift Tear/Void Expulsion.
advice.dps: Stay within the required rift area, dodge circles, and use personals on overlaps.

advice.generic: Grim Chorus moves/activates Notes and causes pulsing Anguish; avoid active Note circles and transition to intermission quickly by silencing Notes.
advice.healer: Spread cooldowns for the escalating pulse damage until the intermission starts.
advice.dps: Use Discordant Beam/assigned mechanics to silence Notes quickly; do not tunnel the boss during ramping chorus.

advice.generic: Rupture is passive AoE from Famished Broken and spawns circles on death; avoid death circles and do not overpull unnecessary pulsing damage.
advice.tank: Move the pack after death circles appear.
advice.healer: Expect background AoE during these packs; it becomes dangerous with Chains or Voidcaller pulses.
advice.dps: Step out of death circles and avoid cleaving yourself with unnecessary stacks.

advice.generic: Gates of the Abyss spawn Umbral Waves; identify gate positions early and keep safe lanes open.
advice.tank: Position the boss so the group can see and dodge all gate wave lanes.
advice.healer: Expect players to spread/move during the gates and avoid being line-of-sighted.
advice.dps: Do not stand between the boss and an active gate wave lane.

advice.generic: Phase Dash creates the circle used to clear Void Bombs; aim it through multiple bombs while avoiding allies.
advice.tank: Stack boss and pets so Phase Dash clears bombs without dragging pets away from cleave.
advice.healer: Top the dash target and group before Overload resolves.
advice.dps: If targeted, route the dash circle through bombs; if not, stay clear and keep cleaving.

advice.generic: Lens Flare targets a player and leaves Blazing Ground; targeted player moves away from allies and places puddles along the edge/previous bad to preserve room.
advice.tank: Keep Viryx positioned so the targeted player can leave without dragging the boss/add out of cleave range.
advice.healer: Spot heal the Lens Flare target and be ready for Scorching Ray overlap.
advice.dps: If targeted, move out decisively and place ground where it will not cut off the group.

advice.generic: During Chakram Vortex, dodge the incoming Gale Surge/Wind Chakram patterns; do not stand in the returning path.
advice.tank: Move Ranjit away from Wind Chakram so melee are not killed by the return path.
advice.healer: Plan healing during Fan of Blades while the group is moving for chakrams.
advice.dps: Track both outgoing and returning chakrams; uptime is secondary to not being one-shot.

advice.generic: Line of sight Searing Quills; use pillars/terrain rather than trying to heal through it.
advice.tank: Keep Rukhran in melee and near usable line-of-sight options without forcing bad Sunwing spawns.
advice.healer: Do not plan to brute-force Quills; help top players before they line.
advice.dps: Move to line of sight early; do not wait for the channel to start ticking.

advice.generic: Gale Surge knocks players back and spawns wind orbs; pre-position so the knockback does not send you off the platform or into hazards.
advice.tank: Position the boss so the group has safe knockback direction and melee is away from returning chakrams.
advice.healer: Be ready to heal while players are displaced and dodging wind orbs.
advice.dps: Check your back before the cast and use movement if the knockback path is unsafe.

advice.generic: Solar Nova is a large AoE from Solar Construct; get out of range and use a personal if you cannot escape in time.
advice.tank: Keep constructs positioned so melee have an exit path for Nova.
advice.healer: Expect avoidable spikes when players are trapped by other trash.
advice.dps: Move out early; this is not a soak.

advice.generic: Wind Blast explodes around Raging Squall; run away before the explosion and use defensives during Wrathful Wind overlaps.
advice.tank: Avoid dragging the pull into corners where players cannot escape Wind Blast.
advice.healer: Prepare spot healing during Wrathful Wind and after late Wind Blast exits.
advice.dps: Leave melee early for the explosion; greed is expensive here.

advice.generic: Solar Fire targets a player with fire damage; use a personal/combat drop if available and avoid overlapping with Solar Nova.
advice.healer: Spot heal Solar Fire targets during construct pulls.
advice.dps: Use defensives or combat reset if targeted and low.

advice.generic: Defensive Protocol sends heat waves; dodge the waves while continuing to soak solar beams as assigned.
advice.tank: Keep Araknath in melee and face Fiery Smash away so wave dodging remains possible.
advice.healer: Top players before Supernova and spot heal beam soakers.
advice.dps: Dodge waves first; soak beams only when safe and assigned.

advice.generic: Flaming Updraft targets two players; move to the outside of the arena to drop puddles because they become Flaming Twisters during Burning Gale.
advice.tank: Keep Emberdawn positioned so outside puddle drops do not cut off melee or tank movement.
advice.healer: Updraft targets may need spot healing, then prepare for heavy intermission damage.
advice.dps: If targeted, place the puddle outside/edge, then return; do not drop it in the center.

advice.generic: Use Turbulent Arrows from Arrow Rain to launch over/avoid Bullseye Windblast; pre-position and avoid doubling up on the same arrow.
advice.tank: Put your back near an arrow for Tempest Slash if needed to cancel knockback and clear Squall Leap stacks.
advice.healer: Watch assigned arrow usage so you can keep range and avoid duplicate launches.
advice.dps: Pick/ping an arrow early and use it for Windblast; do not steal another player’s planned arrow.

advice.generic: Flaming Twisters spawn from previous Updraft puddles during Burning Gale; dodge the moving twisters and Fire Breath frontals.
advice.tank: Expect Fire Breath to start on you then rotate counter-clockwise; orient the boss and move accordingly.
advice.healer: Use cooldowns during Burning Gale because movement, twisters, and frontals overlap with heavy party damage.
advice.dps: Keep moving through safe gaps; do not tunnel during the intermission.

advice.generic: Bladestorm fixates a player during Commander Kroluk add phases; fixated player kites while the group cleaves adds and maintains interrupts.
advice.tank: Collect adds and keep Chain Lightning mobs grouped while the fixated player kites.
advice.healer: Watch the fixated player and tank/add damage simultaneously.
advice.dps: If fixated, kite through safe space; otherwise cleave adds and keep kicks assigned.

advice.generic: Arrow Rain creates Turbulent Arrows; avoid random impacts, then use assigned arrows intentionally to clear Squall Leap or cross Windblast safely.
advice.tank: Preserve arrow positions needed for Tempest Slash knockback handling.
advice.healer: Track who has Squall Leap stacks and who needs an arrow clear.
advice.dps: Use arrows intentionally, not accidentally; communicate if you take one.

advice.generic: Splattering Spew targets players with puddles; loosely spread along the outside and overlap old puddles only if healthy/defensive is active.
advice.tank: Keep Derelict Duo positioned so outside puddle drops do not split the boss pair.
advice.healer: Watch players intentionally overlapping puddles and be ready before Bone Hack.
advice.dps: Place puddles along the edge; only overlap if you can survive it.

advice.generic: Gunk Splatter leaves avoidable sludge/puddles; move out and avoid placing it in the group’s route.
advice.tank: Move enemies out of splatter so melee are not forced into it.
advice.healer: Watch for players slowed/boxed by sludge effects.
advice.dps: Drop/avoid splatter away from the pack and path.

advice.generic: Phial Toss creates an AoE and puddle; move out and avoid placing future movement through the puddle.
advice.tank: Move the pull if Phial Toss lands in melee space.
advice.healer: Spot heal late movers and avoid standing in puddles while casting.
advice.dps: Sidestep immediately and resume damage from safe ground.

advice.generic: Fetid Spew cleaves and drops puddles; loosely spread and place puddles out of the main path.
advice.tank: Move the Flesh Behemoth out of puddles and use a defensive for Brutal Chop.
advice.healer: Expect melee/tank damage plus spew movement in the same pulls.
advice.dps: Do not stack when Behemoth is casting; drop puddles away from the pack.

advice.generic: Arrow Rain zones create the arrows needed to handle Restless Heart mechanics; dodge impacts but keep usable arrows available.
advice.tank: Use arrows to manage knockback/stacks when assigned.
advice.healer: Plan movement so you can heal after using an arrow launch.
advice.dps: Avoid impact circles, then use assigned arrows for Windblast/Squall Leap.

advice.generic: Gore Whirl is a channeled AoE; move out or use CC to stop the channel entirely.
advice.tank: Avoid holding the mob where melee cannot leave the whirl.
advice.healer: Watch melee who are slow to exit.
advice.dps: Stop it with CC if available; otherwise leave the AoE.

advice.generic: Acidic Demise indicates poison/acid death or impact pressure; step out of the acid zone and avoid killing mobs where the group must stand.
advice.tank: Move mobs before they die if their death effect will cover melee.
advice.healer: Watch melee/tank if acid death effects overlap with other damage.
advice.dps: Do not stand in death puddles; reposition before finishing the mob.

advice.generic: Ferocious Pounce is a targeted leap; loosely spread and use a personal if targeted during other damage.
advice.tank: Keep mobs grouped so pounces do not drag enemies far from cleave.
advice.healer: Spot heal the pounce target if it overlaps bleeds or group AoE.
advice.dps: Do not stack tightly when pounce mobs are active.

advice.generic: Freezing Trap is avoidable crowd control; do not step into traps unless specifically using one for a planned mechanic.
advice.healer: Be ready to dispel/help if a player is trapped during damage.
advice.dps: Watch your feet; losing control to a trap during interrupts is costly.

advice.generic: Intimidating Shout requires standing near at least one ally; form small pairs/groups rather than a full stack if that is safer.
advice.tank: Keep the boss stable so melee/ranged can form safe pairs without crossing hazards.
advice.healer: Make sure isolated players know to pair before the cast.
advice.dps: Find one nearby ally before the cast; do not be alone.

advice.generic: Gust Shot targets a player; spread to avoid cleaving allies and overlap existing puddles when safe because it can clear them from the arena.
advice.tank: Keep the boss positioned so Gust Shot clears useful puddles without forcing the group into bad space.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target and watch if they choose to overlap a puddle intentionally.
advice.dps: If targeted, move out and aim/overlap existing puddles when safe.

advice.generic: Interrupt Monotonous Lecture whenever possible; treat missed casts as preventable pressure rather than normal healer load.
advice.healer: Prepare recovery if the cast goes through, but the main answer is prevention.
advice.dps: Hold or assign interrupts so this cast does not complete.

advice.generic: Interrupt Arcane Bolt whenever possible; treat missed casts as preventable pressure rather than normal healer load.
advice.healer: Prepare recovery if the cast goes through, but the main answer is prevention.
advice.dps: Hold or assign interrupts so this cast does not complete.

advice.generic: Arcane Bolt is a spare-interrupt random-target nuke; kick it when higher priority casts are covered.
advice.healer: Spot heal targets if casts get through.
advice.dps: Use spare interrupts after Polymorph/Pyroblast/Terror Wave priority casts are handled.

advice.generic: Pyroblast must be interrupted; the mob is CC-immune, so assign real kicks rather than relying on stops.
advice.healer: Prepare spot healing only if a kick is missed; successful play is preventing the cast.
advice.dps: Keep an interrupt rotation and do not waste kicks on low-priority casts when Pyroblast is coming.

advice.generic: Terror Wave should be interrupted every time; if it goes off, be ready for the dangerous debuff/CC and possible line-of-sight handling.
advice.healer: Dispel quickly if the cast completes and creates a removable debuff.
advice.dps: Prioritize this over filler casts; assign kicks before the pull if multiple Void Infusers are present.

advice.generic: Necrotic Convergence is only interruptible after the shield is broken; damage the shield first, then kick the channel.
advice.healer: Use cooldowns if the shield break is slow; damage ramps with time.
advice.dps: Do not kick early into the shield phase; save the interrupt for when the channel can actually be stopped.

advice.generic: Shadow Bolt is lower priority than Hex/Spirit Rend but should be kicked with spare interrupts to reduce random target damage.
advice.healer: Spot heal targets if casts get through.
advice.dps: Use spare kicks here after priority control casts are covered.

advice.generic: Spirit Rend should be interrupted as often as possible; if it lands, the Magic DoT needs to be dispelled/healed.
advice.healer: Dispel successful Spirit Rend casts quickly, especially on players already low.
advice.dps: Prioritize kicks on Spirit Rend; missed casts turn into healer dispel debt.

advice.generic: Nullify must be interrupted every cast; missed casts are a major wipe risk in both trash and boss add waves.
advice.tank: Position Nullifiers where melee/ranged interrupts can reach them.
advice.healer: Be ready if a cast slips, but the correct play is prevention through kicks.
advice.dps: Assign kicks and hold one for Nullify instead of spending all stops on filler damage.

advice.generic: Holy Bolt is a random-target cast that should be interrupted consistently, especially in light-wing pulls.
advice.healer: Spot heal targets if Holy Bolt gets through while Burning Radiance is active.
advice.dps: Use kicks on Holy Bolt; do not let repeated bolts drain healer capacity.

advice.generic: Plungegrip should be interrupted or stopped; freedom-style utility can also save the target if the grip lands.
advice.healer: Do not treat it as a normal dispel; heal or call for freedom if the target is controlled.
advice.dps: Kick/stop the channel, or use freedom/root-break utility on the target.

advice.generic: Icy Blast is a hard-hitting tank cast; interrupt it consistently.
advice.tank: Use a defensive if a cast is missed; it hits extremely hard.
advice.healer: Be ready for tank spikes if interrupts fail.
advice.dps: Assign kicks; this is a real priority cast, not filler.

advice.generic: Netherburst is a large AoE cast and should be interrupted every time.
advice.healer: Prepare group healing only if a cast slips.
advice.dps: Hold interrupts for Netherburst when Arcanist Cadavers are active.

advice.generic: Mind Blast targets the tank and should be interrupted on rotation to reduce heavy damage.
advice.tank: Use defensives when interrupts are missed or during Collapsing Void movement.
advice.healer: Track missed kicks as tank danger windows.
advice.dps: Maintain kick rotation; do not spend kicks on lower priority casts while Mind Blast is due.

advice.generic: Dread Screech needs assigned interrupts, ideally with ranged coverage for overlaps with Phase Dash.
advice.healer: Prepare group healing if a screech slips through.
advice.dps: Assign at least two interrupts and save a ranged kick for overlap casts.

advice.generic: Summon Voidcaller must be interrupted every cast; letting extra Voidcallers spawn adds heavy group damage.
advice.tank: Keep Dark Conjurors in interrupt range and avoid moving them out of melee kicks.
advice.healer: Prepare only if a summon gets through; preventing the add is the real answer.
advice.dps: Hold kicks for Summon Voidcaller and assign backups.

advice.generic: Abyssal Enhancement should be interrupted every time or purged immediately if it lands.
advice.tank: Use defensives if an empowered mob reaches you before the purge.
advice.healer: Call for purge if you see the buff land.
advice.dps: Kick the cast; if missed, purge the buff before it amplifies damage.

advice.generic: Blinding Light should be interrupted to prevent dangerous group disruption.
advice.healer: Be ready for recovery if a cast gets through, but prevention is the goal.
advice.dps: Prioritize Blinding Light casts from Sun Priestesses.

advice.generic: Solar Blast should be interrupted on High Sage Viryx.
advice.healer: Prepare spot healing only when a kick is missed.
advice.dps: Hold kicks for Solar Blast; do not spend all interrupts on lower-priority casts.

advice.generic: Repel should be interrupted to avoid a mass knockback, especially near platform edges.
advice.tank: Position mobs away from edges when Repel casters are active.
advice.healer: Expect fall/knockback mistakes if Repel is missed.
advice.dps: Kick Repel; this is a control/safety cast, not filler.

advice.generic: Pulsing Shriek shields the caster and becomes a group-damage race; focus the mob, break the shield, then interrupt ASAP.
advice.healer: Use cooldowns while the shield is active and group damage is pulsing.
advice.dps: Hard swap during the shield and interrupt as soon as it becomes kickable.

advice.generic: Poison Blades must be interrupted every time to prevent dangerous poison/tank pressure.
advice.tank: Use defensives if Poison Blades lands and watch for poison stacks.
advice.healer: Prepare poison dispel/spot healing only if the kick is missed.
advice.dps: Hold kicks for Poison Blades; do not let cutthroats free-cast it.

advice.generic: Chain Lightning must be interrupted every cast, especially during Kroluk add waves.
advice.tank: Group mystics so kicks/cleave can reach them quickly.
advice.healer: Be ready for group damage if Chain Lightning slips through.
advice.dps: Assign kicks to mystics and keep them locked down during Rallying Bellow.

mechanics.name: Polymorph
mechanics.existingNote: CC from a missed interrupt; dispel quickly if the cast completes.

advice.generic: Polymorph is preventable with an interrupt; if it lands, remove it immediately so the player is not locked out during mechanics.
advice.healer: Magic dispel Polymorph quickly unless a more lethal dispel is happening at the same time.
advice.dps: Interrupt the cast; if a teammate is polymorphed and you can dispel, help immediately.

mechanics.name: Ethereal Shackles
mechanics.existingNote: Boss Magic root. Dispel or break the root around overlapping mechanics.

advice.generic: Ethereal Shackles roots players during movement-heavy moments; remove with Magic dispel, freedom, or root break.
advice.tank: If shackled as tank, use a root break or call for dispel so you can keep boss/puddles positioned.
advice.healer: Dispel shackled players quickly, especially before Arcane Expulsion or ground effects force movement.
advice.dps: Use personal root breaks/freedom if available; otherwise call it and avoid wasting movement tools while rooted.

mechanics.name: Consuming Void
mechanics.existingNote: Dangerous Magic debuff; dispel promptly, use defensives if applications overlap.

advice.generic: Consuming Void is a dangerous Magic debuff; remove what you can and use personals if too many are active for one dispel cooldown.
advice.healer: Dispel the highest-risk target first and heal/externals through remaining debuffs.
advice.dps: Use personals if you are not the first dispel target; do not assume every application can be instantly removed.

mechanics.name: Holy Fire
mechanics.existingNote: Magic DoT; dispel promptly to limit avoidable damage.

advice.generic: Holy Fire is a Magic DoT from trash; dispel promptly to keep random damage from compounding.
advice.healer: Cleanse Holy Fire when it lands unless saving dispel for a more lethal mechanic.
advice.dps: Interrupt/stop the caster where possible and use off-dispel utility if your class can remove Magic.

mechanics.name: Devouring Strike
mechanics.existingNote: Large tank healing absorb; heal through it rather than scoring as a cleanse.

advice.generic: Devouring Strike is a tank healing absorb, not a cleanse. The answer is mitigation, self-healing, externals, and focused healing.
advice.tank: Use a defensive/self-healing before or during the hit so the absorb does not bury the healer.
advice.healer: Commit focused healing into the absorb quickly; do not waste a dispel expecting it to clear.
advice.dps: Use stops or damage reduction utility to reduce follow-up pressure while the tank absorb is being healed off.

mechanics.name: Ethereal Shackles
mechanics.existingNote: Magic root; dispel or root-break to avoid being pinned in mechanics.

advice.generic: Ethereal Shackles pins the target; remove it before overlapping movement mechanics.
advice.tank: If you are rooted, call for freedom/dispels before tank movement is required.
advice.healer: Prioritize dispel when the root would trap someone in Arcane Residue, beams, or knockbacks.
advice.dps: Break yourself out if your class can; do not wait in a lethal overlap.

mechanics.name: Umbral Splinters
mechanics.existingNote: Discovered from logs (cleared by Detox). Magic debuff; dispel promptly.

advice.generic: Umbral Splinters is a dangerous removable debuff; dispel promptly and use a personal if dispel is delayed.
advice.healer: Prioritize the target if the debuff overlaps group damage.
advice.dps: Use self-dispel/defensive utility if your class can answer it.

mechanics.name: Throw Axe
mechanics.existingNote: Random-target bleed; bleed-cleanse or defensive.

advice.generic: Throw Axe is a random-target bleed; use bleed cleanse or a personal, and control axethrowers to reduce repeated casts.
advice.healer: Spot heal bleed targets and call for bleed clears where available.
advice.dps: Use bleed removal/personal and chain AoE CC to delay additional axe casts.

mechanics.name: Soul Torment
mechanics.existingNote: Dangerous Magic DoT; dispel promptly.

advice.generic: Soul Torment is a dangerous Magic DoT; dispel quickly before it combines with other group damage.
advice.healer: Prioritize Magic dispel and spot heal while it is active.
advice.dps: Use personal/self-dispel if available while waiting for cleanse.

mechanics.name: Puncturing Bite
mechanics.existingNote: Tank bleed; bleed-cleanse utility or defensive coverage.

advice.generic: Puncturing Bite is tank bleed pressure; remove with bleed cleanse where available or cover with defensives.
advice.tank: Use active mitigation/defensives and call for a bleed clear at high stacks or overlaps.
advice.healer: Track it as tank pressure and external during overlaps.
advice.dps: Use bleed-removal utility if available.

mechanics.name: Shred Flesh
mechanics.existingNote: Tank bleed + healing absorb; bleed-cleanse or heal through.

advice.generic: Shred Flesh is a tank bleed plus healing absorb; tank needs defensives/self-healing and bleed cleanse where available.
advice.tank: Use mitigation and self-healing; call for bleed cleanse if available.
advice.healer: Heal through the absorb quickly and external if a bleed clear is not available.
advice.dps: Use bleed cleanse/support utility if your class can remove it.

mechanics.name: Infected Pinions
mechanics.existingNote: Disease that adds group damage; prioritize disease dispels.

advice.generic: Infected Pinions is a disease that adds group damage; remove it quickly when disease dispel is available.
advice.healer: Prioritize disease dispels here because leaving it up increases party pressure.
advice.dps: Use personal disease removal if you have it and healer dispel is overloaded.

mechanics.name: Hex
mechanics.existingNote: CC from a missed interrupt; dispel immediately if it completes.

advice.generic: Hex is avoidable with an interrupt; if it lands, dispel it immediately so the player is not CC’d during mechanics.
advice.healer: Magic dispel Hex quickly unless another dispel is more lethal.
advice.dps: Kick every Hex cast and help dispel if your class can.

mechanics.name: Rending Gore
mechanics.existingNote: DB2: no bleed/dispel mechanic — unavoidable DoT, heal through (NOT a bleed-cleanse target despite the name).

advice.generic: Rending Gore is unavoidable tank DoT pressure, not a bleed-cleanse target despite the name.
advice.tank: Use defensives for applications and avoid assuming a cleanse will remove it.
advice.healer: Heal through it and plan externals if stacks/overlaps become dangerous.
advice.dps: Help reduce tank pressure with stops, slows, or off-healing rather than looking for a bleed dispel.

mechanics.name: Ritual Firebrand
mechanics.existingNote: Magic debuff that erupts repeatedly; dispel quickly to reduce group pressure.

advice.generic: Ritual Firebrand erupts repeatedly if left up; move circles away and remove the Magic debuff quickly.
advice.healer: Prioritize Firebrand dispels during Hex Guardian pulls because passive AoE is already high.
advice.dps: Move out with the debuff and use a personal if dispel is delayed.

mechanics.name: Spirit Rend
mechanics.existingNote: Dangerous Magic DoT from a missed interrupt; dispel promptly.

advice.generic: Spirit Rend is a dangerous Magic DoT from a missed cast; remove promptly.
advice.healer: Dispel the target unless another lethal dispel is active.
advice.dps: Use a defensive/self-dispel if your kick was missed and dispel is delayed.

mechanics.name: Hooked Snare
mechanics.existingNote: DB2: root + bleed. Freedom/root-break for the root; bleed-cleanse for the bleed component.

advice.generic: Hooked Snare is a root/bleed-style control problem; freedom/root-break the root and use bleed removal if your class can handle the bleed component.
advice.healer: Do not treat this as a normal healer cleanse unless your class can remove the relevant effect; help the target survive if they are stuck.
advice.dps: Interrupt or freedom the target; if you are hooked, call it and use movement/root break utility.

mechanics.name: Frost Nova
mechanics.existingNote: Magic root; dispel or root-break when movement is required.

advice.generic: Frost Nova is a Magic root; dispel or root-break it when the target needs to move.
advice.healer: Cleanse it quickly if the player is in danger or near Lost Souls.
advice.dps: Use your own root break if available; do not sit rooted in the path of mechanics.

mechanics.name: Transference
mechanics.existingNote: Redirects healing to the caster; immediate dispel — leaving it up invalidates healing.

advice.generic: Transference redirects healing into the caster; dispel immediately because leaving it up makes healing the player ineffective.
advice.healer: This is an emergency Magic dispel. Clear it before committing throughput into the target.
advice.dps: Call it if you have it and use a defensive until the dispel lands.

mechanics.name: Suppression Field
mechanics.existingNote: Snare. DB2 puts the snare mechanic on the parent cast (1269283), not this aura id, so removableBy is overridden here. Freedom / movement utility clears it; not a healer dispel.

advice.generic: Suppression Field is a snare handled by freedom/movement utility, not a normal healer dispel.
advice.healer: Do not waste a cleanse expecting this to behave like Magic; heal the target and call for freedom if needed.
advice.dps: Use movement-clearing utility if you have it; otherwise minimize movement and avoid stacking on the group.

mechanics.name: Burning Radiance
mechanics.existingNote: Dangerous Holy DoT; dispel promptly.

advice.generic: Burning Radiance hits two players; dispel one quickly and heal/defensive the other until the next dispel is available.
advice.healer: Remove one immediately, then commit healing to the second target.
advice.dps: Use a personal if you are not the first dispelled target.

mechanics.name: Creeping Void
mechanics.existingNote: Curse that increases Shadow damage taken; remove before more Shadow damage lands.

advice.generic: Creeping Void is a curse that increases Shadow damage taken; remove it before more shadow damage lands.
advice.healer: If your healer cannot remove curses, call for DPS curse utility.
advice.dps: Use curse removal on affected players quickly; this is not just healer responsibility.

mechanics.name: Fan of Blades
mechanics.existingNote: Boss bleed; bleed-cleanse or defensive coverage.

advice.generic: Fan of Blades bleed is dangerous; use defensives and bleed cleanse where available.
advice.healer: Use major healing during Fan of Blades and track bleed-cleansable targets.
advice.dps: Use a personal or bleed removal; do not add avoidable damage during the bleed.

mechanics.name: Blade Rush
mechanics.existingNote: Bleed from Blade Rush; bleed-cleanse or defensive.

advice.generic: Blade Rush leaves a bleed; remove with bleed cleanse where available or use a personal during overlaps.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target if bleed cannot be removed.
advice.dps: If targeted, use defensive/bleed cleanse and avoid stacking additional hits.

mechanics.name: Curse of Torment
mechanics.existingNote: Curse with a healing absorb + Shadow DoT; remove promptly where a curse dispel is available.

advice.generic: Curse of Torment is a curse with a healing absorb and Shadow DoT; remove it if curse dispel is available, otherwise heal the absorb off.
advice.healer: Call for curse dispel if your class cannot remove it; commit healing if no curse removal is available.
advice.dps: Use curse removal on yourself/allies when possible. This saves healer throughput.

mechanics.name: Permeating Cold
mechanics.existingNote: Magic frost DoT + heavy slow; dispel OR clear with movement utility (snare purge clears the whole aura).

advice.generic: Permeating Cold is a Magic frost DoT/slow; dispel it or clear with freedom/snare-removal utility.
advice.healer: Dispel one target quickly and call for freedom/self-clears on the other if multiple are active.
advice.dps: Use snare clear/freedom-style utility if you have it; do not sit slowed in ground mechanics.

mechanics.name: Rotting Strikes
mechanics.existingNote: Discovered from logs (cleared by Burn It Out). Stacking max-health-reduction disease; dispel.

advice.generic: Rotting Strikes stacks reduce max health; disease dispel early or the tank becomes much easier to kill.
advice.tank: Watch stacks and kite if disease removal is unavailable or delayed.
advice.healer: Disease dispel the tank before stacks make normal hits lethal.
advice.dps: Use disease removal/off-utility if available and help control Rotlings.

mechanics.name: Plungegrip
mechanics.existingNote: Physical grip channel; interrupt or Freedom-style utility, not a healer cleanse.

advice.generic: Plungegrip is physical control handled by interrupt/freedom, not healer cleansing.
advice.tank: Position so a gripped player is not dragged through lethal ground.
advice.healer: Heal the target and ask for freedom/interrupt rather than spending cleanse globals.
advice.dps: Stop the channel or free the target with utility.

mechanics.name: Cryoshards
mechanics.existingNote: Discovered from logs (cleared by Cleanse/Detox). DB2: Magic + snare; dispel or clear with movement utility.

advice.generic: Cryoshards is a Magic snare/damage effect; dispel or clear with movement utility before it causes a failed movement check.
advice.healer: Prioritize targets who need to move for Glacial Overload or dodge patterns.
advice.dps: Self-clear if possible and avoid using mobility into the snare without removing it.

mechanics.name: Rotting Strikes
mechanics.existingNote: Discovered from logs. Stacking max-health-reduction disease; dispel.

advice.generic: Rotting Strikes is stacking max-health reduction; remove disease stacks before they snowball.
advice.tank: Call stack count and use defensives/kiting while waiting for cleanse.
advice.healer: Cleanse promptly; do not let stacks sit through a tank buster.
advice.dps: Control or burst Rotlings to limit new applications.

mechanics.name: Shadow Pounce
mechanics.existingNote: Short dangerous bleed; bleed-cleanse where available.

advice.generic: Shadow Pounce is a short dangerous bleed; remove with bleed cleanse where available or use a personal.
advice.healer: Spot heal the target if no bleed removal is available.
advice.dps: Use bleed cleanse/personal on yourself or allies where possible.

mechanics.name: Chains of Subjugation
mechanics.existingNote: DB2: snare (not root). Freedom/movement utility clears it; not a dispel.

advice.generic: Chains of Subjugation hits the whole group and snares; loosely spread, use personals/group defensives, and clear snare with freedom/movement utility.
advice.tank: Do not pull extra Subjugators together unless the group has cooldowns; multiple Chains overlaps can be lethal.
advice.healer: Pre-plan cooldowns for Chains and be ready to heal absorbs shortly after.
advice.dps: Spread enough not to stack hits; use personal/freedom and avoid blaming healers for stacked chains.

mechanics.name: Backstab
mechanics.existingNote: Bleed on a player; bleed-cleanse where available, else defensive.

advice.generic: Backstab applies a bleed to a player; use bleed cleanse if available, otherwise defensive/spot healing.
advice.healer: Track the bleed when it overlaps group AoE.
advice.dps: If targeted, use a personal and call if you have no cleanse available.

mechanics.name: Rift Essence
mechanics.existingNote: Magic debuff that increases Shadow damage taken; dispel before more Shadow damage.

advice.generic: Rift Essence increases Shadow damage taken; remove it before additional Shadow mechanics hit.
advice.healer: Magic dispel high-risk targets before Void Expulsion, Waves, or boss shadow damage.
advice.dps: Use self-dispel/racial if available and avoid extra shadow hits while debuffed.

mechanics.name: Peck
mechanics.existingNote: Stacking tank bleed; bleed-cleanse, else defensives/CC/kite.

advice.generic: Peck is a stacking tank bleed from eagles; reduce stacks with control, kiting, or bleed-cleanses when available.
advice.tank: Watch stacks and avoid chaining new eagle casts into other tank busters; kite or call for bleed removal when stacks climb.
advice.healer: Tank damage can spike as stacks build; external or spot heal if no bleed removal is available.
advice.dps: Help stop or control eagle casts and use bleed-cleanse utility if your class has it.

mechanics.name: Lasher Toxin
mechanics.existingNote: Stacking poison that ramps fast; rotate poison dispels aggressively.

advice.generic: Lasher Toxin stacks quickly once lashers are active; use poison removals and control to keep stacks from snowballing.
advice.tank: If stacks climb, kite controlled lashers and call for poison dispels or stops before Barkbreaker/other tank damage overlaps.
advice.healer: Prioritize poison dispels on the tank or highest-stack target; do not wait for stacks to become unhealable.
advice.dps: Use stuns, slows, knockbacks, and burst AoE to reduce the number of lashers applying stacks.

mechanics.name: Vile Bite
mechanics.existingNote: Tank bleed; bleed-cleanse where available, else defensive.

advice.generic: Vile Bite is tank bleed pressure; treat high stacks or overlaps as dangerous rather than normal melee damage.
advice.tank: Use defensives/active mitigation and kite if the bleed stacks are getting ahead of healing.
advice.healer: Track the bleed and use externals or heavy spot healing if no bleed cleanse is available.
advice.dps: Use bleed removal, stuns, or slows to reduce tank pressure where possible.
